v.n. A defect or vice to show itself (in a horse); to show vice, to be vicious (a horse)
(-par, or -ko), To impute fault, &c. (to), to blame, to accuse (of); to defame, asperse, disparage, &c.; — to accuse of fornication or adultery
(-meṅ), To pick holes (in), point out the defects or faults (of), to find fault (with)
adj. Without fault, or flaw, or blemish, &c.; sound, faultless.

def: adj. & s.m. (H. fem. ʻaiban), Unsound, faulty, defective; vicious; infamous; — one-eyed; squint-eyed; — a vicious person, a bad character.

part. adj. Returning, coming back, reverting (to), relating or referring (to, — as a pronoun to a preceding noun), appertaining or belonging (to); coming (to), turning (towards), arriving (at), visiting, alighting, resting; resulting; brought back, being restored (as money lent, &c.); repeating; beginning (to be), becoming; happening, befalling, betiding; accustomed or habituated (to); liable (to), subject, amenable, or exposed (to)
subs: v.n. To come (upon, -ko), to befall, betide, happen (to); to visit; to alight, rest (upon); to be liable (to), &c.

ety: Pehl. dīn, Zend daêna, rt. dī = S. धी
def: s.m. Regulation, institute, statute, rules, law (as established by princes, in contradistinction to sharʻ or the law of Moḥammad), body of laws, code; enactment, edict, ordinance, canon, decree, rule; custom, manner

def: s.m. The eye; sight; source, fount, fountain; udder (of an animal); the choice, the best, the very essence (of a thing), the thing itself; money, cash; gold; the sun; the letter ʻain (the fourteenth of the Arabic, and twenty-fourth of the Urdū alphabet)
subs: adj. (As) 'the letters ʻain and g̠ain,' but slightly different, very much alike; — squint-eyed; — having a cataract
s.m. Principal, capital; net profit; land-revenue
adj. & adv. The very, the precise; the very same, exactly alike
s.m. The very or precise moment; opportune moment, the nick of time; — ʻain waqt-par, adv. At the very time, in the very nick of time; punctually to the moment
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عَیْن (ayn), from Arabic عَيْن (ʕayn).

r: ʻain, ʻain-ě-mǒhmala, ʻain-ě-gair-manqūt̤a, alif, laʻl, lāl, hamza
def: (called ʻain-ě-mǒhmala, or ʻain-ě-gair-manqūt̤a), is the twenty-fourth letter of the Urdū alphabet (the eighteenth of the Arabic). This letter is peculiarly Arabic or Semitic, and occurs only in such Hindūstānī words as are borrowed from the Arabic, and in the secondary formations from these; or in the arabicized form of Persian words in which it has replaced an original alif (e.g. لعل laʻl, for لال lāl). It is a strong (but, to Europeans and most other people, unpronounceable) guttural, related in its nature to the hamza, and ḥ; and is described as produced by a smart compression of the fauces and forcible emission of breath. By the common people of India it is sounded like alif. As a numeral it stands for 70.
s.m. Mirror, looking-glass; the knee-pan; — adj. Mirror-like, clear, bright, &c.
subs: adj. Adorned with mirrors; — āʼīna-band karnā, To adorn with mirrors
s.m. Mirror-holder (an officer at Eastern courts); — (met.) a mendicant; — āʼīna-dārī, s.f. Holding a mirror; the office of mirror-holder
To show the looking-glass or mirror (as an Indian barber does on his rounds, when he wishes to know if his services are required); to hold up the mirror to one, to show one what he is
To look at the face in a glass
adj. Having a face as bright as a mirror
s.m. Mirror maker or manufacturer; — āʼīna-sāzī, s.f. The trade of mirror-making; the manufacture of looking-glasses
s.m. A mirror of polished steel (attributed to Alexander)
s.m. An instrument for polishing mirrors
s.m. An apartment, the walls of which are covered with mirrors; mirror-chamber.

def: s.m. The two eyes, both eyes.

ety: ʻain+P. dim. aff. ak = S. क
def: s.f. Glasses to assist the sight, spectacles.

ety: aiṅć˚ = Prk. आयंछ(इ) or आइंछ(इ) = S. आकर्क्ष्य(ति), the fut. (used in the sense of the present) of rt. आ+कृष्
def: v.t. To draw, pull, drag, attract; to string up, hang (usually aiṅć-denā); to draw or rule (a line, &c.); to write, scribble; to sketch; to keep or hold back or aloof
subs: adv. With difficulty, by hook or by crook
v.t. To draw out; draw up, draw in, imbibe, absorb, suck up; to take, take in; to take away by force, carry off (usually eṅć-le-jānā), wring from, extort.

def: adj. Rolling; lolling; idle, useless, unserviceable, worthless; incomplete, unfinished
subs: v.t. To render useless or unserviceable; to injure; to break
v.n. To become useless, worthless, unserviceable, &c., to get out of order, be injured, broken, spoiled; to be unfinished or incomplete.

def: s.m. A make-weight (placed in the lighter scale).
adj. (f. -ī), Staggering, rolling, reeling, swaggering, mincing; crooked, zigzag; harsh, unseemly, rude, coarse (speech, &c.)
subs: v.t. To call names, to abuse, villify.

def: = H اينڐانا ऐंडाना aiṅḍānā, (caus. of aiṅḍnā, q.v.) To cause to roll or sway from side to side, &c.; to put in the scale, to weigh roughly.
= H اينڐالنا ऐंडालना aiṅḍālnā, (caus. of aiṅḍnā, q.v.) To cause to roll or sway from side to side, &c.; to put in the scale, to weigh roughly.
v.n. To strut, stalk, swagger ( = aiṅṭhnā); to roll or turn from side to side, to roll about (as in bed); to stagger or reel (like a drunkard); to lie idle, loll about.
adj. Familiar with or relating to magic, magical, deceptive, illusory; — s.m. Magician, juggler.
adv. Here, hither.
adj. Of or relating to the eye; eye- (in comp., e.g. gawāh-ě-ʻainī, 'eye-witness'); — having a blemish in the eye, rejected (as a horse).

ety: for A. عينية, fem. fr. عيني ʻainī
def: adj. Of or relating to the eye or eyes.

ety: S. आवृत्त+कः, or from aiṅṭnā, q.v.
def: s.m. Twist, turn, convolution ( = aiṅṭan, aiṅṭhan, aiṅṭh), clew, hank, skein, &c. (syn. kalāba); the string of a spinning wheel on which the spindle works, cross-strap round wheels; — a snail.

s.f. = aiṅṭā, aiṅth, aiṅṭhan, q.q.v. s.m. Twist, turn, convolution ( = aiṅṭan, aiṅṭhan, aiṅṭh), clew, hank, skein, &c. (syn. kalāba); the string of a spinning wheel on which the spindle works, cross-strap round wheels; — a snail.
s.f. Twist, turn, convolution, coil, fold, ply; tightening; stiffness, rigidity; obstinacy, stubbornness; pride, arrogance; affectation, airs, strut (syn. akaṛ, q.v.)

s.f. Twist, &c. ( = aiṅṭh, q.v.); constipation; gripes, colic. s.f. Twist, turn, convolution, coil, fold, ply; tightening; stiffness, rigidity; obstinacy, stubbornness; pride, arrogance; affectation, airs, strut (syn. akaṛ, q.v.)
v.t. To twist, turn, wring; to cause to writhe; to cause to strut, &c.

v.t. To twist, wind, spin, make into hanks or skeins; to make crooked, to distort; to wring (the ear); to punish, chastise; to tighten, squeeze, press, contract; to wrench from, extort, take by force or fraud, appropriate wrongfully; — v.n. To twist, writhe, wriggle; to cramp; to be tightened, cramped, pressed, to shrink, be contracted; to become rigid, stiff, &c.; to strut, stalk, &c. ( = akaṛnā, q.v.)
subs: adv. Stiffly, rigidly; affectedly, with a strut, proudly, &c. ( = akaṛ-kar)
v.t. (intens. of aiṅṭhnā), To twist tight, &c.; to wring from, extort, &c.: see aiṅṭhnā.

def: v.t. & n. To twist, &c. = aiṅṭhnā (the more common form).
s.f. Petulance, peevishness; pride.
s.m. Proud, &c. = aiṅṭhū, akṛū, akaṛ-bāz, q.q.v.
s.m. Pride; affectation, &c. = akaṛ, q.v. s.m. (in Gram.) The letter अ a.

ety: Pehl. drafsh; Zend drafsha; S. drapsa
def: s.m.(?), A banner, ensign, standard; — a sash wound round the head or the helmet when going to battle; — light, splendour; lightning; anything glittering, shining, or flashing; — a cobbler's awl ( = darosh, q.v.).
s.m. This one and that one; anyone and everyone (without distinction); stranger; a nobody-knows-who.
s.m. Indra's elephant.
s.f. Name of the river Rāvī (in the Panjāb).

adj. & adv. (f. -ī), This-like, such, of this sort; like ( = sā, in which case it is subjoined to the accusatival base of a pronoun, and to the acc. or gen. case of a substantive, e.g. āp-aisā 'aqlmand, 'a wise man like you'; mard-aisā, 'like a man'; par-kī aisī lakīroṅ ke rūp, 'in the form of feathery streaks'); in such a manner, in this wise, thus, so; after this sort, of a piece; — as though, as if; — so much, this much ( = itnā)
subs: adj. Such and such, so-so, &c. = aisā-waisā, q.v.; — s.m. (f. aisī-taisī), Such an one, a nondescript, disreputable person; one destitute of discrimination, or of carefulness

adj. & adv. (f. aisī-waisī), So-so, passable, middling; indifferent, of little moment or worth, insignificant, inferior, poor, bad; improper, indecent (act or language); — indifferently, passably, &c. ( = aise waise)
adv. Thus, so, &c. = aisā; — also = aise-hī, q.v.
aise, intj. (colloq.) Really! is it so! surely not! you don't say so! what next!
s.f. Disgrace, infamy, ignominy; a polite term for anything unmentionable
adj. Just so, precisely; — as if, as though, so to speak; — accidentally, casually; without motive or reason ( = yūṅ-hī).

def: adj. & adv. (dialec.) = aisā, q.v. adj. & adv. (f. -ī), This-like, such, of this sort; like ( = sā, in which case it is subjoined to the accusatival base of a pronoun, and to the acc. or gen. case of a substantive, e.g. āp-aisā 'aqlmand, 'a wise man like you'; mard-aisā, 'like a man'; par-kī aisī lakīroṅ ke rūp, 'in the form of feathery streaks'); in such a manner, in this wise, thus, so; after this sort, of a piece; — as though, as if; — so much, this much ( = itnā)
subs: adj. Such and such, so-so, &c. = aisā-waisā, q.v.; — s.m. (f. aisī-taisī), Such an one, a nondescript, disreputable person; one destitute of discrimination, or of carefulness

adj. & adv. (f. aisī-waisī), So-so, passable, middling; indifferent, of little moment or worth, insignificant, inferior, poor, bad; improper, indecent (act or language); — indifferently, passably, &c. ( = aise waise)
adv. Thus, so, &c. = aisā; — also = aise-hī, q.v.
aise, intj. (colloq.) Really! is it so! surely not! you don't say so! what next!
s.f. Disgrace, infamy, ignominy; a polite term for anything unmentionable
adj. Just so, precisely; — as if, as though, so to speak; — accidentally, casually; without motive or reason ( = yūṅ-hī).

def: adv. See s.v. aisā. adj. & adv. subs: adj. Such and such, so-so, &c. = aisā-waisā, q.v.; — s.m. (f. aisī-taisī), Such an one, a nondescript, disreputable person; one destitute of discrimination, or of carefulness

adj. & adv. (f. aisī-waisī), So-so, passable, middling; indifferent, of little moment or worth, insignificant, inferior, poor, bad; improper, indecent (act or language); — indifferently, passably, &c. ( = aise waise)
adv. Thus, so, &c. = aisā; — also = aise-hī, q.v.
aise, intj. (colloq.) Really! is it so! surely not! you don't say so! what next!
s.f. Disgrace, infamy, ignominy; a polite term for anything unmentionable
adj. Just so, precisely; — as if, as though, so to speak; — accidentally, casually; without motive or reason ( = yūṅ-hī).

ety: inf. n. of عيش 'to live'; 'to live a pleasant life'
def: s.m. (orig.) 'Life; animal life'; a life of pleasure and enjoyment, pleasure, delight, luxury; gratification of the appetites, sensuality; carnal intercourse
subs: or ʻaish karnā, v.n. To enjoy oneself; to revel in pleasure, or in sensual enjoyments
s.m. The slave of pleasure; a sensualist, a sybarite
s.f. or ʻaish-maḥal, s.m. Place or house of enjoyment or pleasure; — ʻaish-maḥal, Banqueting-house; — the inner or women's apartments of the house of a person of wealth or rank
s.m. Joy and delight, pleasure, merriment, excessive pleasure
s.f. or ʻaish-o-nashāt̤, s.m. Pleasantness of life; luxurious enjoyment; sensual pleasures, voluptuousness, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عَیْش (ayš), borrowed from Arabic عَيْش (ʕayš), from عَاشَ (ʕāša).

def: adj. Made of reeds.
adv. This year.

s.m. Control, dominion, sway, supremacy, power, might; grandeur, glory, pomp, wealth, prosperity, greatness, majesty, state; superhuman power, omnipotence (of the Deity); the divine faculties of omnipresence, invisibility, &c.
adj. Endowed with superhuman qualities; grand, glorious, majestic, &c.

v.n. = aiṅṭhnā, q.v. v.t. To twist, wind, spin, make into hanks or skeins; to make crooked, to distort; to wring (the ear); to punish, chastise; to tighten, squeeze, press, contract; to wrench from, extort, take by force or fraud, appropriate wrongfully; — v.n. To twist, writhe, wriggle; to cramp; to be tightened, cramped, pressed, to shrink, be contracted; to become rigid, stiff, &c.; to strut, stalk, &c. ( = akaṛnā, q.v.)
subs: adv. Stiffly, rigidly; affectedly, with a strut, proudly, &c. ( = akaṛ-kar)
v.t. (intens. of aiṅṭhnā), To twist tight, &c.; to wring from, extort, &c.: see aiṅṭhnā.

def: intj. See s.v. ai. s.m. The letter or sound ai.
s.m. Hall, gallery, portico (applied generally to a royal palace).

ety: from ewaṛ and wārā = S. wāṭakah
def: s.m. A shed in a jungle for goats, sheep, or cattle.
adj. Having the menses ( = kapṛon-se).
adj. Of time; for a long time; durable.

ety: intens. n. fr. عير 'to move to and fro'
def: adj. & s.m. Sharp, artful, shrewd, cunning, sly, mischievous (syn. ćatur); — an artful or crafty fellow, a knave.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عیار (iyār), from Arabic عِيَار (ʕiyār).

def: s.f. An artful woman, a sly or cunning jade.
s.f. = H عيارپن ʻaiyār-pan, s.m. = H عيارپنا ʻaiyār-panā, (abst. s. fr. ʻaiyār) = ʻaiyārī, q.v.

ety: ʻaiyār+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Artfulness, slyness, cunning, craftiness, knavery, imposture; — (in Urdū) an artful woman ( = ʻaiyārnī, or ʻaiyāra).
s.f. An artful woman, a sly or cunning jade.
s.m. = H عيارپنا ʻaiyār-panā, = P عيارگي ʻaiyāragī, s.f. (abst. s. fr. ʻaiyār) = ʻaiyārī, q.v.
= H عيارپن ʻaiyār-pan, s.m. = P عيارگي ʻaiyāragī, s.f. (abst. s. fr. ʻaiyār) = ʻaiyārī, q.v.

ety: intens. n. fr. عيش 'living,' inf. n. of عيش
def: adj. & s.m. Living well, luxurious, jovial, voluptuous, addicted to pleasure; libidinous, lecherous; — one who is addicted to pleasure, a voluptuary, sensualist, debauchee, rake.

ety: ʻaiyāsh, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Luxury, luxuriousness, joviality; the being addicted to pleasure, voluptuousness, sensuality, debauchery.
s.m. Quantity; number; value.
prop. n. Job.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَیُّوب (ayyūb), from Arabic أَيُّوب (ʔayyūb).

def: s.m. The star Capella, the bright star (a) upon the left shoulder of Auriga.
(acc. of aiẓ, 'doing a thing again'), adv., Again, as above, as before, item, the same, ditto, in the same manner.
adj. pl. (of عزيز ʻazīz), Very excellent, worthy, or dear, most excellent, most dear.

ety: v.n. fr. عوج 'to be crooked or curved,' &c.
def: s.m. Ivory.
adv. To-day, this day; this time, now
subs: adv. Up to this day; to this time, till now; hitherto; as yet
adv. To-day by day, to day
adj. (f. -ī), To-day's, this, of the present; new, recent, fresh; — āj-kī rāt, This night, to-night
āj-ke roz, adv. To-day — āj-kal, adv. To-day or to-morrow; in a few days, in a day or two, soon; in these days, now-a-days; recently, of late
āj-kal karnā, v.n. To put off from day to day, to procrastinate, delay; to shilly shally, evade, shuffle
adj. (f. -ī), Of the present day, age or generation; recent, fresh
ajahūṅ, adv. (emphatic), 1˚ = āj-tak, q.v. 2˚. This very day, to-day even; just now, even now, at this very time; immediately.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀅𑀚𑁆𑀚 (ajja), from Sanskrit अद्य (adyá).

def: s.f. A she-goat; the unreality of the universe, illusion (an epithet of Māyā, considered as the wife of the Supreme Being).
s.m. (f. -ī), Paternal grandfather
subs: s.m. A guru's guru, a grandguru.

def: s.m. Wonder; astonishment; admiration; a wonderful thing; — adj. Wonderful, marvellous, astonishing, amazing, miraculous, strange, extraordinary, rare; droll, &c.
subs: v.n. To wonder, to be astonished, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عَجَب (ajab), from Arabic عَجَب (ʕajab).

def: = اجہون ajahūṅ, q.v.s.v. آج āj. v.s.v. آج āj.
v.s.v. آج āj.
adj. & s.m. Wonderful, strange, extraordinary; — wonders, wonderful things, curiosities
subs: s.m. The wonders of the creation; monsters
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عَجَائِب (ajā'ib), from Arabic عَجَائِب (ʕajāʔib), plural of عَجِيبَة (ʕajība).

def: (pl. of ʻajāʼib), s.m. pl. = ʻajāʼib, q.v. adj. & s.m. Wonderful, strange, extraordinary; — wonders, wonderful things, curiosities
subs: s.m. The wonders of the creation; monsters

def: part. adj. Overtaken by fate, doomed; on the point of death; wretched, unfortunate
subs: adj. = ajal-rasīda; and s. com. gend. One who is overtaken by fate; one whose hour of death is at hand; doomed person, &c.
wety: From Classical Persian اَجَل (ajal), from Arabic أَجَل (ʔajal).

ety: for A. عجلة, inf. n. of عجل 'to make haste'
def: s.f. Haste, speed, velocity, &c.

ety: comp. and super of جليل jalīl
def: adj. Greater; greatest; more, or most, glorious.
wety: From Classical Persian اَجَل (ajal), from Arabic أَجَل (ʔajal).

ety: v.n. fr. عجم 'to have an impediment, or a difficulty, &c. in speaking Arabic
def: s.m. Foreigners, others than Arabs; barbarians; Persians; — Persia.
adj. Foreign; Persian; barbarian.
s.m. The name of a certain Brāhman mentioned in the Rāmāyaṇ who was a great sinner and a penitent.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Unknowing, ignorant, simple, artless, innocent; indifferent, careless; — a simpleton
subs: adj. Ignorant of business, or of any particular work.

def: v.s.v. آنا ānā.
s.f. Ignorance; simplicity, innocence; heedlessness.
adj. (f. -ā), Unborn, unbegotten.
adj. (f. -ā), Ending in a short vowel.
s.m. Agent.
s.f. Agency; the office of a political agent or resident at a Native court; the residence of the same officer, the residency.
s.m. Lit. 'That does not wear out'; cloth made of hemp; sacking; coarse canvas. (Syn. ṭāṭ).
adj. Not liable to decay or old age, undecaying, imperishable; an epithet of God; — ever youthful or young; young, vigorous; firm, immovable
subs: ajar-amar, adj. Exempt from both decrepitude and death; an epithet of God.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Disreputable; infamous; — disrepute, disgrace, dispraise; detraction, defamation; bad name; a disreputable person, &c.
subs: ajas lenā, To get a bad name, incur blame, &c.

def: adj. & adv. Perpetual; perpetually, for ever.
s.m. One who has lost caste; one who has no caste.
s.m. One who has lost caste; one who has no caste.
s.f. As much as will fill the cavity produced by placing the open hands, slightly hollowed, side by side; perquisites of the lower castes from the threshing floor.
s.f. Advances, particularly to agricultural labourers. (Cf. agauṛī.)
adj. Not victorious; unsuccessful; defeated, beaten; subdued; — unvanquished; invincible; — s.m. Defeat
(pl. of جد jadd), Ancestors, forefathers. (See ābā.)
wety: Borrowed from Arabic أَجْدَاد (ʔajdād), plural of جَدّ (jadd).

def: s.m. = اژدها azhdahā, q.v. s.m. A large serpent, python, boa constrictor ( = ajgar); a dragon.
adj. Very heavy or ponderous; unwieldy.
ex: اجگر ایک مامولی سانپ سے زیادہ خطرناک ہوتا ہے۔
ajgar ek māmūlī sā̃p se zyāda xatarnāk hotā hai.
The python happens to be more dangerous than a run-of-the-mill snake.

def: adj. Wonderful, marvellous, strange, surprising
subs: wonder, marvel, prodigy; evil, harm, wrong; violence, outrage, oppression, tyranny ( = غضب g̠aẓab; in these senses ajgut would appear to be identical with ajugat or ajukt = S. ayukta).

def: adj. More, or most, ignorant, very ignorant: — s.m. A blockhead.
adj. Lit. 'Not shaking or swinging'; of steady gait or pace (applied to an elephant).

ety: S. अ interj.+जी, contr. fr. जीव
def: Interj. (used in addressing, or to call attention, or to express surprise, or contempt), ho there! I say; Sir! O sir! Good sir! Madam! My lady; good woman or good man (forms used by a husband and wife respectively in addressing each other, it being considered disrespectful for them to call each other by name).
s.f. (from ājā), A grandmother.

ety: v.n. fr. عجب 'to wonder'; see ʻajab
def: adj. Inducing wonder or admiration, wonderful, surprising, astonishing, marvellous, admirable, strange, extraordinary, rare, unique.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عجیب ('ajīb), from Arabic عَجِيب (ʕajīb). Compare Punjabi ਅਜੀਬ (ajīb) / عَجِیب (ʻajīb).

ety: for A. عجيبة, fem. of عجيب
def: adj. & s.m. = ʻajīb, and ʻajūba, qq.v. adj. Inducing wonder or admiration, wonderful, surprising, astonishing, marvellous, admirable, strange, extraordinary, rare, unique.
part. adj. Hasting, making haste; speedy, quick, agile, expeditious; — quickly passing away, fleeting, transitory.
adj. & s.m. Great; illustrious; great men; the great; the illustrious.
s.m. The hairy skin of an antelope, tiger, &c. which serves the religious student for a couch, seat, &c.
s.m. Embryos, fœtuses.
s.m. An area, court.
s.m. Hired labourer or servant, hireling, mercenary; slave.

adj. Not decomposed; unimpaired; undigested; — indigestible; unwholesome, injurious, pernicious; disagreeable, distasteful, sickening, nauseating, wearying, wearisome, tedious, sick or weary (of); persistent, unremitting; inveterate, chronic (as a disease); excessive (so as to produce nausea, disgust, &c.); hardy, strong; — s.m. Surfeit, indigestion; flatulence; nausea, &c.
subs: To be sickening, nauseating, wearying, &c.; to be surfeited; to be glutted (lit. & fig.); to be produced in abundance, to abound.

def: part. (f. -ā), Unconquered, unsubdued, unsurpassed; invincible.
adj. Unconquered, unsubdued; unsurpassed; invincible
subs: (S. ajīta+varṇa), s.m. Leprosy of a bad or incurable kind.

def: s.m. Adjutant (of which it is a corr.).
adj. Lifeless; deprived of life.
s.m. = S اجيوکا अजीविका ajīvikā (vulg. ājīvkā,ājīʼukā, ajīukā), s.f. Means of subsistence; livelihood; subsistence; food.
s.f. = S آجيو आजीव ājīv, s.m. Means of subsistence; livelihood; subsistence; food.

ety: act. part. of عجز 'to lack strength, to be powerless,' &c.
def: adj. Lacking strength or power, or ability, powerless, impotent, unable (to do), unequal (to); weak, feeble, helpless; brought low, overcome; lowly, humble; exhausted; dejected; in despair, hopeless; baffled, frustrated
subs: or ʻājiz rahnā, or ʻājiz honā (-se), To be helpless (against); to be unequal (to), to be unable to do, to fail or fall short (of, or in respect of); to despair (of), &c.
v.t. To render (one) powerless or helpless; to bring low, to humble, &c.

def: (for A. عاجزة, fem. of ʻājiz), adj. f. = ʻājiz, q.v. adj. Lacking strength or power, or ability, powerless, impotent, unable (to do), unequal (to); weak, feeble, helpless; brought low, overcome; lowly, humble; exhausted; dejected; in despair, hopeless; baffled, frustrated
subs: or ʻājiz rahnā, or ʻājiz honā (-se), To be helpless (against); to be unequal (to), to be unable to do, to fail or fall short (of, or in respect of); to despair (of), &c.
v.t. To render (one) powerless or helpless; to bring low, to humble, &c.

ety: ʻājiz, q.v. + Zend aff. āna or ana = S. अन
def: adv. Impotently, weakly, helplessly, humbly; hopelessly.

ety: ʻājiz+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Inability, powerlessness, helplessness; humility, submissiveness; supplication, entreaty; hopelessness
subs: (-se), To make humble entreaty (to), to implore, beseech, to supplicate.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عَاجِزِی (ājizī), from Arabic عَاجِزٍ (ʕājizin). By surface analysis, عَاجِز ('ājiz, “humble”) + ی (ī)

def: s.m. Base, vile, mean, or ignoble people; the rude, the vulgar; the lower orders.
adj. More, or most, beautiful; perfect.
s.m.f.? Lit. 'goat's delight'; parsley (Apium involucratum); the common carraway (Carum carui); a kind of lovage (Ligusticum ajwan).
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit अजमोद (ajamoda). Doublet of اجمودا.

ety: pron. aggya in E. and N.W. India
def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Not knowing, ignorant, stupid, idiotic; unknown; — an ignorant person, &c.: — adv. Unwittingly.
s.f. Command, order ( = ḥukm), injunction, precept; permission, leave
subs: adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Law-breaking, refractory, disobedient; — a law-breaker, &c.
(f. -ī), and ājṅā-pālak, adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Law-regarding, obedient; — an obedient person
s.m. Written order or authority; edict, warrant, commission
(i.e. ˚nā+ādh˚), adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Amenable to command or authority, tractable, docile, dutiful; dependent; subservient; a tractable person, &c.
(or ajṅā-), kār, adj. Executing an order, obeying, obedient; ministrant; — s.m. (f. -ī), A servant
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Commanding, ordering; — commander; ruler
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Carrying out orders, obedient; ministrant; obsequious; — an obedient person
v.t. To command; to rule; to issue the order (for, -kī); to execute the order (of)
s.m. Disregarding command or authority; transgression; disobedience
adj. & s.m. (f. -vatī). Brought under control or subjection, subdued, subjected; — obedient person; subject
adj. & sm. (f. -inī), Mindful of or attentive to orders; obedient; — one who obeys orders.

def: (aggyā), 1˚ = next; 2˚ (fem. of ajna), an ignorant woman, &c.

ety: from جنب, 'to put at a distance
def: s.m. Stranger, alien; foreigner; — adj. Strange; foreign; new; unknown.

ety: from جنب, 'to put at a distance
def: s.m. Stranger, alien; foreigner; — adj. Strange; foreign; new; unknown.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اجنبی (ajnabī), from Arabic أَجْنَبِيّ (ʔajnabiyy).

def: s.f. Strangeness; the circumstance or state of being a foreigner.
s.m. Non-cognizance; ignorance, stupidity, folly
s.f. = ajṅānpan, q.v. s.m. Ignorance, stupidity, folly.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Ignorant; unwise, foolish; — an ignoramus.
s.m. Ignorance, stupidity, folly.
s.f. Kinds, sorts, species; various sorts; goods, chattels, effects; articles, commodities.
adj. (f. -ā), Unknown; unexpected; — unknowing; unsophisticated, simple
subs: (S. ajnāta+yauvanā), s.f. A girl arrived at maturity and surprised at the symptoms which then appear.

def: s.f. = اجنانپن ajṅānpan, q.v. s.m. Ignorance, stupidity, folly.
v.s.v. آج āj.
adj. Without joining or connection; disconnected, disjointed, incoherent, incongruous; not matching; ill-matched, ill-sorted; unpaired; inharmonious; unsuitable, — unequalled, matchless, peerless.
adj. Without lustre; not bright; dull, dim.
adj. Uncultivated, untilled, waste (land).
s.m. Recompense, compensation, reward ( = s̤awāb), remuneration, return, requital, retribution; cost, price, hire, fare, pay, fee
s.m. Bodies (inanimate, — and thus distinguished from ajsām, q.v.); the heavenly bodies.
s.m. Bodies. (Syn. badan.)
s.m. Bodies ( = badan); material substances

ety: redup. of bājū = P. بازو bāzū
def: adv. Round about, around, about, in the vicinity ( = ās-pās).
s.m. = آيدهہ āyudh, q.v. s.m. Weapon, armour, arms.
part. adj. (f. -ā). Not suited, ill-matched; unfit, unsuitable; incompatible
s.f. A mother; (in theatrical language) a courtesan.
part. adj. (f. -ā). Not suited, ill-matched; unfit, unsuitable; incompatible
adj. Hasty, quick, expeditious, &c. (i.q. ʻājil, q.v.). part. adj. Hasting, making haste; speedy, quick, agile, expeditious; — quickly passing away, fleeting, transitory.
s.f. Unpaid labour.
s.m. Compensation; remuneration; wages, hire; rent; fare
subs: s.m. A labourer for hire; hireling (not a regular servant)
s.f. Wages; hire; payment by the job or piece.

def: adj. Untasted, untouched (food).

= اجوان ajwān, q.v. s.f. The seed of a plant of the dill kind (Ligusticum ajowan); a species of lovage or bishop's weed with the flavour of carraways (Ptychotis ajowan), used medicinally by the natives. (For varieties of the plant v.s.v. چهوري ćhorī, and خراساني ḵẖurāsānī).
s.f. A kind of caudle given to puerperal women (Also written aćhwānī.)

ety: S. यवानी; — cf. H. forms ajwāʼin, and jwāʼin
def: s.f. The seed of a plant of the dill kind (Ligusticum ajowan); a species of lovage or bishop's weed with the flavour of carraways (Ptychotis ajowan), used medicinally by the natives. (For varieties of the plant v.s.v. چهوري ćhorī, and خراساني ḵẖurāsānī).

ety: for ajwad, compar. & super. of جود
def: adj. Better; best; very good; excellent.

ety: inf. n. of عجز 'to lack strength or power,' &c.
def: s.m. Powerlessness, impotence, weakness, helplessness, submission, wretchedness (ct. ʻājizī).
s.m. Parts, portions, divisions, sections; sections of the Qorʼān; feet (of a verse); constituent parts (of a thing), elements; members; ingredients (of any compound or mixture).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اجزا (ajzā), from Arabic أَجْزَاء (ʔajzāʔ).

def: s.m. Curled, flowered, gigantic swallow-wort, Calotropis gigantea (syn. madār).
s.m. A sprout of sugar-cane (cf. ankur).
s.m. Lord, master, owner ( = اغا āg̠ā. q.v.). s.m. Curled, flowered, gigantic swallow-wort, Calotropis gigantea (syn. madār).
= H اکا akkā, s.m. Lit. 'elder brother' ( = آغا dg̠ā); brother; friend, dear friend; dear sir.
s.m. pl. (of اکبر akbar), The great, grandees, people of rank
subs: High and low, rich and poor.

def: s.m. pl. (of ʻaqīda), Articles of faith, religious tenets.
s.m. One of the sacks or baskets of a pannier, &c. = اکها akkhā or ākhā, q.v.
s.m. Hurt, injury, loss, detriment; wrong, impropriety; uselessness; — adj. Useless, unprofitable, vain, worthless ( = akājī); — adv. Uselessly, unprofitably, in vain.
adj. & s.m. Detrimental, injurious, retarding, obstructive; useless; — an obstructive person or thing, a useless person; an impediment; a retarder.
v.t. To inflict injury; do a wrong; — to render useless; — v. int. To die.
s.m. Acacia; the expressed juice of the fruit of the Acacia tree, or of the qulqul (or qilqil).
s.m. = آکار ākār, q.v. s.m. (in Gram.) The letter अ a.

s.m. An inopportune or unseasonable time; untimeliness, unseasonableness; a bad season, a season of scarcity; drought, scarcity, dearth, famine; extremity, pinch; adj. Untimely, unseasonable, inopportune, premature
subs: s.m. Unseasonable fruit, fruit produced out of season
s.f. Untimely or premature death; sudden death
ex: دیس میں بہت بھیانک اکال پڑا ہے۔
des mẽ bahut bhayānak akāl paṛā hai.
There is a terrible famine happening in the nation.

def: adj. & adv. Sole, alone, by oneself, himself, &c. (used in compn.)
subs: (ikal-), -khurā, adj. (f. -ī), Eating alone or by oneself; greedy, selfish; unsociable, inhospitable; — s.m. A greedy person; selfish person; jealous or envious person.

def: adj. Without stain, without rust; spotless, unblemished, pure.
s.m. pl. (of اقليم iqlīm), Climes, regions.
s.m. The least degree or quantity; the lowest computation; a minimum
s.m. Naturalness, genuineness, trueness; — adj. Natural, genuine, true.
part. adj. Not wrought or manufactured, inartificial, natural.
adj. Unpropitious, unlucky, ominous; uncomfortable, disconsolate, unhappy, unblessed.
adj. Useless, unprofitable, &c. = اکارتهہ akārath, q.v. adj. Unprofitable, fruitless, yielding no return, serving no purpose, ineffectual, vain; bootless, useless, unserviceable
subs: akārath honā, v.n. To go for nothing, serve no purpose; to be thrown away, be lost, wasted, sacrificed to be bootless, be in vain; to afford no enjoyment
v.t. To render useless or unavailing; to mar, ruin, destroy; to give to waste or to the winds, to waste, squander, dissipate; to spend to no purpose.

def: adj. Without desire; — s.m. An epithet of the Supreme.
s.m. Firmness, steadiness.
s.m. Shaking, trembling.
adj. Unshaken, firm.
adj. Shaking, trembling,
s.m. Grass and weeds collected from a ploughed field.
s.f. Wish, desire; hope, expectation; purpose, intention.
adj. & s.m. Wishful, desirous; hopeful, expectant; asking, inquiring; — an expectant.
part. adj. Desired, wished for, expected; desirous.
= اکننا akannā, q.v.
v.n. To listen (to), give ear (to), to attend, hear, hearken.
adj. Free from thorns; free from difficulties; free from enemies; thornless, unimpeded, unobstructed.
s.m. (in Gram.) The letter अ a.
adj. = اکارتهہ akārath, q.v. adj. Unprofitable, fruitless, yielding no return, serving no purpose, ineffectual, vain; bootless, useless, unserviceable
subs: akārath honā, v.n. To go for nothing, serve no purpose; to be thrown away, be lost, wasted, sacrificed to be bootless, be in vain; to afford no enjoyment
v.t. To render useless or unavailing; to mar, ruin, destroy; to give to waste or to the winds, to waste, squander, dissipate; to spend to no purpose.

def: adj. Exempt from duty, duty-free, exempt from taxes; — hand-less.
s.m. (f.?), Accumulation, collection, multitude; a rich source (of anything), source, spring, mine, quarry; aborigines.
s.f. Hole, den (of a wild beast).

s.f. Twist, bend, crookedness, obliquity, contortion, distortion; stiffness, rigidity, inflexibility; hardness, firmness, fixedness; obduracy, intractableness; wilfulness, obstinacy, stubbornness, doggedness; — constipation, costiveness; — strut, airs, show, affectation, foppery, parade, ostentation; pride, conceit, arrogance
subs: s.m. An affected person, fop, swaggerer
s.f. Affectation, strutting, swaggering, airs, foppishness; ostentation, show; pride, haughtiness
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi اکڙ (akṛ /⁠akaṛ⁠/) (c. 1679), from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀆𑀓𑁆𑀓𑀟 (*ākkaḍa). Cognates Cognate with Punjabi آکَڑ / ਆਕੜ (ākaṛ), Pahari-Potwari آکَڑ (ākaṛ) and Marathi अकड (akaḍ).

def: s.m. The letter आ ā; — appearance, aspect; form, shape, figure, feature, countenance; statue; likeness; — sign, token, hint
past. conj. part. of انا ānā, q.v.
adj. & adv. Causeless, groundless; senseless, useless; wanton; — causelessly, uselessly, &c.
adv. = اکارن akāraṇ, q.v. adj. & adv. Causeless, groundless; senseless, useless; wanton; — causelessly, uselessly, &c.
adj. Unprofitable, fruitless, yielding no return, serving no purpose, ineffectual, vain; bootless, useless, unserviceable
subs: akārath honā, v.n. To go for nothing, serve no purpose; to be thrown away, be lost, wasted, sacrificed to be bootless, be in vain; to afford no enjoyment
v.t. To render useless or unavailing; to mar, ruin, destroy; to give to waste or to the winds, to waste, squander, dissipate; to spend to no purpose.

def: adj. Unprofitable, fruitless, yielding no return, serving no purpose, ineffectual, vain; bootless, useless, unserviceable
subs: akārath honā, v.n. To go for nothing, serve no purpose; to be thrown away, be lost, wasted, sacrificed to be bootless, be in vain; to afford no enjoyment
v.t. To render useless or unavailing; to mar, ruin, destroy; to give to waste or to the winds, to waste, squander, dissipate; to spend to no purpose.

def: (a neg.+perf. part. of karānā), s.f. Lit. 'Not prepared'; ground not prepared and properly cleaned for receiving the seed.
s.m. pl. (of قريب qarīb), Relatives, kindred.
s.m. corr. of ʻāqar-qarḥā, q.v.
adj. Not hard, not rugged; soft, tender.
adv. Stiffly, rigidly; affectedly, foppishly, with a strut, swagger, &c.
adj. Without work, idle.
s.m. A low action, a bad action, sin, wickedness, vice.
